name: Baked Salmon with Dill
tagline: Slow-roasted salmon with lemon, dill, and olive oil
highlight: Low-and-slow keeps salmon silky and never dry — no shellfish

hero.alt: Baked salmon fillet with dill and lemon slices
hero.caption: salmon — low-roasted with dill

healthFacts: Salmon is among the highest food sources of omega-3 EPA/DHA.
Cooking at 275°F keeps the protein from squeezing out white albumin.

steps.title: Bring to room temp
steps.detail: Pull salmon from the fridge 20 min before cooking. Pat dry, salt and pepper.
steps.tip: Cold salmon in a hot oven cooks unevenly.

steps.title: Drizzle and dress
steps.detail: Place salmon skin-down on parchment-lined sheet. Drizzle olive oil, scatter dill and grated garlic, top with lemon slices.
steps.tip: Lemon under the salmon creates a citrus steam — bonus flavor.

steps.title: Slow-roast
steps.detail: Roast at 275°F for 18 min — center should look just opaque and flake when pressed. Pull when slightly underdone; carryover finishes it.
steps.tip: Low temperature is the key to silky salmon. High heat = chalky.

steps.title: Rest and squeeze
steps.detail: Rest 3 min. Squeeze a roasted lemon slice over before serving. Spoon up any pooled juices to drizzle over.
steps.tip: Pooled lemon-dill oil = the dish's best part.
